description: Broad evidence categories for compressing Amazon review histories before mapping to the MatrAIx persona schema.

evidence_categories.label: Product interests
evidence_categories.description: Repeated product categories, titles, genres, hobbies, media, tools, and topics the reviewer engages with.

evidence_categories.label: Consumption preferences
evidence_categories.description: Preferences visible in purchase/review behavior, such as price sensitivity, durability, convenience, aesthetics, quality, brand loyalty, or practical usefulness.

evidence_categories.label: Expertise signals
evidence_categories.description: Technical vocabulary, comparative evaluation, domain-specific criteria, or repeated knowledgeable reviews in a field.

evidence_categories.label: Behavioral habits
evidence_categories.description: Repeated routines or use contexts, such as reading habits, DIY repair, home organization, gifting, cooking, travel preparation, or repeated replacement purchases.

evidence_categories.label: Decision style
evidence_categories.description: How the reviewer evaluates options, such as research-heavy comparison, cautiousness, novelty-seeking, risk avoidance, or tolerance for tradeoffs.

evidence_categories.label: Values and motivations
evidence_categories.description: Grounded priorities stated or repeatedly reflected in reviews, such as learning, reliability, frugality, comfort, productivity, sustainability, creativity, safety, or care for others.

evidence_categories.label: Explicit self-statements
evidence_categories.description: Direct statements about the reviewer, such as job, family role, health context, location, education, identity, or life circumstances. These must be quoted directly and must not be inferred from products alone.

evidence_categories.label: Communication style
evidence_categories.description: Review-writing patterns such as detailedness, directness, emotional tone, critique style, and explanation depth.

direct_only_note: Demographic, health condition, family status, occupation, geography, political affiliation, religious identity, and identity-like attributes require explicit quoted self-statements. Repeated product or topical engagement can be preserved as contextual interests, needs, preferences, or values, but should not be converted into asserted sensitive identity/status labels without explicit evidence.
